metadata.author: Juan Antonio Breña Moral
metadata.title: Java Object-Oriented Design Guidelines
metadata.description: Enum and annotation guidance for expressive, type-safe Java object-oriented design.

prompt.role: You are a Senior software engineer with extensive experience in Java software development.
prompt.goal: Use enums and annotations effectively when the skill orchestration identifies constants, ordinals, bit fields, lookup, or override problems. Read this reference only after `121-java-object-oriented-design` has compiled the project and classified this concern as applicable.

constraints.constraints-description: Apply only guidance relevant to the diagnosed concern and preserve observable behavior.

constraint-list.constraint: Read this reference only when the SKILL.md orchestration maps the request or code problem to this concern.
Apply focused, incremental refactorings and preserve existing business behavior.
Do not broaden the change into unrelated object-oriented design concerns.

example-header.example-subtitle: Effective Use of Enums and Annotations

example.example-description: Enums and annotations are powerful Java features that, when used correctly, can make code more readable, type-safe, and maintainable.

example-header.example-title: Use Enums Instead of Int Constants
example-header.example-subtitle: Replace int constants with type-safe enums

example.example-description: Enums provide type safety, namespace protection, and additional functionality that int constants cannot offer.

example-header.example-title: Use Instance Fields Instead of Ordinals
example-header.example-subtitle: Don't derive values from enum ordinals; use instance fields

example.example-description: Ordinal values can change when enum constants are reordered, making code fragile.

example-header.example-title: Use EnumSet Instead of Bit Fields
example-header.example-subtitle: Replace bit field enums with EnumSet for better type safety and performance

example.example-description: EnumSet provides all the benefits of bit fields with better readability and type safety.

example-header.example-title: Use EnumMap Instead of Ordinal Indexing
example-header.example-subtitle: Use EnumMap for enum-keyed data instead of ordinal indexing

example.example-description: EnumMap is specifically designed for enum keys and provides better performance and type safety.
The @Override annotation catches errors at compile time and makes code more readable.
